name: FlowChart

aliases.name: FCEventConfig
aliases.comment:
Config for subscribing to events

        onCanvasClick -> when canvas is clicked
        onNodeClick FCNode -> when any node is clicked
        onLinkClick FCLink -> when any link is clicked

aliases.name: Model
aliases.type: { position : FlowChart.Types.Vector2, nodes : Dict.Dict String.String FlowChart.Types.FCNode, links : Dict.Dict String.String Link.Model, currentlyDragging : Internal.DraggableTypes, dragState : Utils.Draggable.DragState, targetMsg : FlowChart.Msg -> msg, portConfig : { portSize : FlowChart.Types.Vector2, portColor : String.String }, linkConfig : { linkSize : Basics.Int, linkColor : String.String } }

values.name: defaultLinkConfig
values.comment:
get default link config

    linkSize = stroke width of link (2px)
    linkColor = Color of link (#6495ED)

values.name: defaultPortConfig
values.comment:
get default port config

    portSize = Size of port in Vector2 (20, 20)
    portColor = Color of port (grey)

values.name: initEventConfig
values.comment:
pass list of events to subscribe to.
Currently supported are :
onCanvasClick, onNodeClick, onLinkClick

values.name: loadFlowChart
values.comment:
load flowchart state from a json file. Will open a file selector dialog
    to select the file

        loadFlowChart FlowChartModel

values.name: removeLink
values.comment:
remove link from canvas

        removeLink "link-id" FlowChartModel

values.name: removeNode
values.comment:
remove node from canvas

        removeNode "node-id" FlowChartModel

values.name: saveFlowChart
values.comment:
save flowchart state as json file

        saveFlowChart "<path/to/file.json>" FlowChartModel

values.name: update
values.type: FlowChart.FCEventConfig msg -> FlowChart.Msg -> FlowChart.Model msg -> ( FlowChart.Model msg, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: view
values.type: FlowChart.Model msg -> (String.String -> Html.Html FlowChart.Msg) -> List.List (Html.Attribute FlowChart.Msg) -> Html.Html msg

name: FlowChart.Events
comment:
Listeners for the various flowchart events.

@docs onCanvasClick, onNodeClick, onLinkClick

values.name: onCanvasClick
values.comment:
Register a `CanvasClick` event listener. It will not trigger if canvas has moved
after mouse down.

values.name: onNodeClick
values.comment:
Register a `NodeClick` event listener. It will not trigger if node has moved
after mouse down.

name: FlowChart.Json
FlowChart.Types
comment:
# Defination

@docs Vector2, FCNode, FCPort, FCLink, FCCanvas

aliases.name: FCCanvas
aliases.comment: Canvas data to initialize the flowchart
aliases.type: { nodes : List.List FlowChart.Types.FCNode, position : FlowChart.Types.Vector2, links : List.List FlowChart.Types.FCLink, portConfig : { portSize : FlowChart.Types.Vector2, portColor : String.String }, linkConfig : { linkSize : Basics.Int, linkColor : String.String } }

aliases.name: FCLink
aliases.comment:
Link data to connect nodes through ports

    id = Id of the link, should be unique
    from = NodeId, portId of the start of link
    to = NodeId, portId of the end of link
aliases.type: { id : String.String, from : { nodeId : String.String, portId : String.String }, to : { nodeId : String.String, portId : String.String } }

aliases.name: FCNode
aliases.comment:
Represent the data of a single node in flowchart

    id = unique identifier for node
    position = position of node wrt to position of canvas
    dim = width and height of node
    nodeType = string identifier used to render html from node map
    port = List of ports

        node = FCNode "node-1" (Vector2 20 20) (Vector2 100 100) "default" [
            FCPort "port-1" Vector2 0 0.42
            ,FCPort "port-2" Vector2 0.85 0.42
        ]
aliases.type: { id : String.String, position : FlowChart.Types.Vector2, dim : FlowChart.Types.Vector2, nodeType : String.String, ports : List.List FlowChart.Types.FCPort }

aliases.name: FCPort
aliases.comment:
Nodes are connected through links via Ports. Use a value between [0, 1] for port position

        port = FCPort "port-1" Vector2 0 0.42

aliases.name: Vector2
aliases.comment:
Vector2 which can represent position or dimension of node, canvas, etc.

        pos = Vector2 10 10
